caseTitle: Marcus Bell — The PE and the liver mass
_status_notes: 2 timepoints. TP1 — initial admission (HD5): acute unprovoked PE + extensive DVT + a 17 cm liver mass with portal-vein thrombus, biopsy recommended (Q1 anticoagulation duration before biopsy + rationale; Q2 general biopsy-timing factors; Q3 DOAC hold duration + rationale; Q4 bridging factors). TP2 — readmission ~1 month later: progressed bilateral PE with RV strain after the apixaban hold (Q5 IVC filter for this patient; Q6 general IVC filter pros/cons). Weights 0.9/0.7/0.7/0.5/0.9/0.5 = 4.2 total. Source: Management Case Bank, Case 2 (original discussants Allyson Pishko, Brett Carroll, Jeff Weinstein; finalized by Zahir Kanjee).
description: A 2-stage case following Marcus Bell, a previously healthy 43-year-old man who presents with an essentially unprovoked pulmonary embolism and extensive DVT and is incidentally found to have a 17 cm liver mass with portal-vein thrombus, concerning for malignancy. The case tests competing-risk reasoning: how long to anticoagulate before biopsying the lesion, how to handle a DOAC around an invasive procedure, when (not) to bridge, and — after a recurrent PE occurs during the peri-procedural anticoagulation hold — whether an IVC filter is indicated.
warning: Plan to dedicate about 28 minutes uninterrupted. Pausing is permitted but stops the timer.
instructions: You are caring for this patient across two points in time. Review the chart, then respond to each clinical question. Your responses are graded against a rubric; the case's intended teaching points are revealed at the end.
originalDiscussants: Allyson Pishko, Brett Carroll, Jeff Weinstein
